math_recheck: Re-derived every load-bearing step against arXiv 1207.6365 LaTeX source: Bernstein (V<=LT^2/6), W=T log(t/dh)+r/t, Hanson-Wright Q<=sqrt(lW) with ||B||_F^2<=W and ||B||_2<=W, birthday s^2/t, Khintchine cross-term <=C_p W, net (grid E, gamma=1-1/sqrt2, |E|<=e^{cr}, J=U^T S^T S U - I), dimension t=O((r/eps)^4 log^2) -> refined O(P(r/eps)^2 log^6) via matrix-Bernstein (rho^2<=2bj/nj+1/nj^2, M<=2bj+1/nj, tau=Theta(q(bj+1/nj+sqrt(r/t)))); corollaries (regression O(nnz)+~O(d^3/eps^2), leverage Pi1=F.S, low-rank affine v=Theta(eps^-4 k^2 polylog) v'=Theta(eps^-3 k polylog) with (1+eps)^2 Delta_k, lp w=Theta(r^6 log n(r+log n))). Independently confirmed the iterative-refinement contraction: |1-sigma^2| max branch is (1+eps0)^2-1=2eps0+eps0^2<=3eps0, contracts iff 3eps0<1; the deliverables' eps0=1/4 is correct and the primary source's own eps0=1/2 (3/2>1) is wrong. Ran the answer.md code: least_squares ratios 1.077/1.020/1.004 at t=400/1600/6400 matching synthesis; leverage_scores recovers true scores (sum~=d); iterative converges to ratio 1.0 once t large enough that eps0<1/3 (diverges at t=400 where sigma_max=1.58>sqrt2, consistent with the stated 3eps0<1 condition).
caveat: No source equation could be obtained from the Sarlos FOCS06 PDF (404 mirror), but its load-bearing facts (Fast-JL dense-solve cost O(t d log d + d^3 eps^-1 log d), AMM) are restated verbatim in the primary source (Thm 12 of s06 cited at nmain.tex:2422; AMM at lem:tail) and were verified there, not from memory.
